# Claude Code prompt ‚Äî [5] Seed a full-feature template tender

*Paste everything below the line into Claude Code. Generated by Cowork, 2026-05-26.*

---

You are working on `GH-Mantova/ProjectOperations`. Read `CLAUDE.md` and follow
it. Add one richly-detailed **template tender** to the seed data ‚Äî a reference
the owner copies from when building real quotes. It must exercise the full
feature set of a quote.

## Approach

- Branch off `main`: `feat/seed-template-tender`.
- Add it to the existing seed. The seed is idempotent / upsert-based per
  `CLAUDE.md` ‚Äî this change is **purely additive**: it must not modify or
  remove any existing tender. Use stable deterministic IDs consistent with the
  existing seed convention.
- It is a **new Tender plus a ClientQuote** ‚Äî the quote/overlay functions need
  a ClientQuote to exercise them. Build both.
- Give it an unmistakable template identity ‚Äî e.g. project name
  "TEMPLATE ‚Äî Full-Feature Reference Quote" ‚Äî so it is obvious on the board.
  Pick a tender ref consistent with the system's numbering.
- No schema change and no migration ‚Äî this is data only.

## What the template must contain

Exercise every quote function:

- **Disciplines & line items** ‚Äî all four discipline groups, each with several
  line items:
  - **DEM (Demolition)** ‚Äî e.g. internal strip-out, structural demolition,
    slab removal, masonry demolition.
  - **CIV (Civil)** ‚Äî e.g. trench excavation, service reinstatement, hardstand
    works.
  - **ASB (Asbestos)** ‚Äî e.g. Class A friable removal, Class B bonded removal,
    air monitoring, Form 65 submission.
  - **Other** ‚Äî and within it **explicitly include concrete cutting, core
    drilling / coring, and concrete grinding / flush-cutting line items** (the
    owner specifically called these out). If the system has a dedicated
    discipline for cutting/coring, place them there instead.
  - Give each line item realistic qty / unit / notes where the model supports
    them.
- **Cost summary** ‚Äî multiple cost lines across the disciplines, with a total.
- **Provisional sums** ‚Äî at least two provisional-sum items.
- **Cost options** ‚Äî at least two optional / alternative cost-option items.
- **Assumptions** ‚Äî several project-specific assumptions; use the richer
  assumption mode (linked to items if the model supports it, otherwise free).
- **Exclusions** ‚Äî several project-specific exclusions.
- **Referenced drawings / documents** ‚Äî a few referenced drawing entries.
- **Overlay flags** ‚Äî on the ClientQuote overlay, turn every show-flag ON
  (`showProvisional`, `showCostOptions`, `showScopeTable`, `showAssumptions`,
  `showExclusions`, `showReferencedDrawings`) and set `detailLevel` to the
  fuller "detailed" setting, so the rendered quote shows the complete feature
  set. (`detailLevel` simple/detailed and the assumption modes are either/or ‚Äî
  set the template to the richer option of each, and state the chosen settings
  in the PR body.)
- **Everything else a quote renders** ‚Äî preliminary works, full T&C,
  signature/acceptance block come from the builder automatically; just ensure
  the tender/quote data does not suppress them.

Reference the `QuoteOverlay` type (in
`apps/api/src/modules/pdf-rendering/builders/quote-html.builder.ts`) and the
Tender / ClientQuote Prisma models for the exact fields. The goal: a quote
that, rendered to PDF, shows every section populated.

## Verify

- Run `pnpm seed` on a dev DB ‚Äî confirm it is idempotent, the new template
  appears, and existing tenders are untouched.
- Generate the quote PDF for the template's ClientQuote and confirm every
  section is populated: cost summary across the four disciplines, provisional
  sums, cost options, scope table including concrete cutting / coring,
  assumptions, exclusions, referenced drawings, T&C, acceptance block.
- Save that PDF to `docs/samples/sample-quote-template-full.pdf` and state the
  path in the PR body so Cowork can smoke-test it.
- Run the ¬ß6 pre-PR CI checklist.

## PR ‚Äî do NOT auto-merge

- Title: `[5] Seed ‚Äî full-feature template tender`
- Body: what the template contains; the overlay settings chosen; the sample
  PDF path; additive seed change (no existing data touched); no migration.
- Reviewer `GH-Mantova`.
- Create the PR, push the sample PDF, state its path, and stop ‚Äî do not enable
  auto-merge. Cowork smoke-tests the rendered template PDF before merge.
